title: kalanchoe campanulata
intro: Kalanchoe campanulata, also known as the lantern flower or firewheel plant, is a succulent species native to Madagascar, characterized by its bright red, bell-shaped flowers and narrow, pointed leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and glycosides. The health benefits of Kalanchoe campanulata include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to treat digestive issues and skin problems. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
